pages.raw_text:
any real choice in the matter, has
made the situation almost laugh
able. But it is serious rather than
humorous, and among the chief pro-
testants is Vice President Conklin
of the North Side Improvement
club, who is backed by the mem
bers of that organization in his
objections.


The objections raised are that
the Second and Washington site is
inaccessible and that it is a loca-
ton where a building would not
add to the general attractiveness of
the city. 1t is too far from the
Washington Water Power lines,
and many persons think this is
too far for women and invalids to
walk, as many who register for
elections will not want to go that
far.


The sites most favored are the
at Second and Howard,
and the Clough and Merriam sites.
A few favor the Auditorium site,
but believe that it is not large
and imposing enough to be the
city's main place for transacting
business for the next few decades.
